codeReview.summary: Fail. The commit adds useful mock-backed coverage for the cross-area contracts, but it does not actually add a repeatable local-real validation asset for a booted `giro` process on `127.0.0.1:8080` tied to one resolved credential source. Both new files stand up in-process test servers with mock upstreams instead of exercising the live local service path the feature description requires.

issues.description: `TestCrossAreaMatrix` explicitly runs against a 'mock-backed server instance' and creates both the Kiro upstream and gateway with `httptest.NewServer`, while the companion server tests do the same via `newCrossAreaRouter` and `newTestAuthManagerWithUpstream` in `internal/server/cross_area_test.go`. That means the committed assets never target a locally booted `giro` process on `127.0.0.1:8080`, never verify startup composition or the selected resolved credential source, and cannot be rerun as the repeatable local-real matrix promised by the feature. Add a real local validation runner/asset that drives the existing service manifest or approved boot command against the live process and captures the full matrix from one process lifetime.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared validation notes record the required matrix and boot commands, but they still do not preserve the concrete request payloads/transcripts needed to rerun the advanced local matrix (tool use, vision, cross-protocol same-run) without reconstructing them from a worker transcript.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/var/home/milton/Workspaces/M/giro/.factory/library/user-testing.md:18-40` lists the required matrix and boot commands, but the reusable asset added in this feature is only the mock-backed `test/e2e/cross_area_test.go:59-100`. The worker transcript for session `0143b1de-d656-4729-b023-387c1848dae1` shows several ad hoc curl commands for the live matrix, and none of those payloads were committed as a reusable repo-side asset.

summary: Review failed: the feature adds mock-backed cross-area tests, but it does not commit a repeatable live local validation asset for the real `127.0.0.1:8080` matrix tied to one resolved credential source.
